title: Breadth-First Search, Queues, and Unweighted Shortest Paths · Behavior Case: Bfs Changed Cases
summary: The queue uses slow front deletion, predecessors change after discovery, distances mix cells and pixels, and shortest is claimed on weighted edges. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bfs changed cases increment in deque-based shortest-path solver. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.
Explain why BFS minimizes edge count only when every admitted passage has equal cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_bfs_shortest_path_5(reported_edges: int = 18, oracle_edges: int = 18, discovered: int = 39, predecessor_links: int = 38, fifo_order_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1:
        return False, reported_edges, "bfs-count"
    if reported_edges != oracle_edges:
        return False, reported_edges, "nonshortest-route"
    if predecessor_links != discovered - 1:
        return False, predecessor_links, "predecessor-map"
    if not fifo_order_valid:
        return False, discovered, "frontier-order"
    assert reported_edges == oracle_edges
    return True, reported_edges, "shortest-unweighted-route"

options.text: For repair the bfs changed cases defect, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Bfs Changed Cases, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the bfs changed cases defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Changed Cases model · Theory Bfs Changed Cases
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Bfs Changed Cases: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 2156 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Changed Cases model · Theory Bfs Changed Cases.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Changed Cases model · Theory Bfs Changed Cases, keep Bfs Changed Cases connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Changed Cases model · Theory Bfs Changed Cases, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Bfs Changed Cases precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Changed Cases model · Theory Bfs Changed Cases, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2156 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Changed Cases model · Theory Bfs Changed Cases, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: One perfect maze is enough to test BFS.
